demos.report: EXAMINATION: CT BRAIN PERFUSION W RECON CLINICAL HISTORY: left m1 occlusion  COMPARISON:  None TECHNIQUE: Whole brain CT perfusion was performed after administration of IV contrast. Post processing was performed. Dose lowering technique(s) such as automated exposure control, iterative reconstruction, and mA and/or KV adjustment for patient size  was utilized for this examination. FINDINGS: The CTA of the head demonstrates large occlusive thrombus in the M1 and M2 segment of the left MCA with collateral cortical leptomeningeal circulation. The CT of the head demonstrates acute ischemic changes in the left basal ganglia and left corona  radiata with aspect score of about 8. Hypoperfusion volume in the left MCA territory (Tmax > 6 s, includes core and penumbra): 40 mL Infarct core volume (CBF <30% of contralateral): 0 mL Mismatch (penumbra) volume: 40 mL Mismatch ratio: Infinite Other: None.  IMPRESSION:  1. Perfusion deficit involving the left MCA with infarct core volume of 0 mL, mismatch volume of 40 mL, with a mismatch ratio of infinite. 1RM1RADPS82

FINDINGS:  Left-sided cord infarct by CBF less than 30% is 332 mL. Left-sided hypoperfusion by Tmax greater than 6 seconds is 343 mm. Mismatch volume is 11 mm. mismatch ratio is 1.  IMPRESSION:  Left-sided infarct and hypoperfusion as described above. 1RM1RAD_PS02

signature.instructions:
Label acute/recent stroke territory from radiology text.

/no_think

Return final answer only. Do not include hidden reasoning, scratchpad,
chain-of-thought, analysis, self-correction, or explanation.

Return only allowed labels. Use NONE only when the report has no
qualifying acute/recent/current territory evidence. Prefer the smaller
label set when evidence is ambiguous, and never include NONE with a real
territory label.

fields.description: A JSON-style list of labels chosen only from allowed_labels. Return labels only; no reasoning.
